text:
Hello, readers! In today’s evening digest, News18 brings to you the latest updates on France’s high-speed rail network being affected, NTA releasing revised scorecard of NEET-UG 2024 exam and other stories.

Arsonists Hit France Rail Network Ahead Of Olympics Opening As Cancellations Trigger Chaos: All You Need To Know

Arson attacks disrupted France’s high-speed rail network on Friday, affecting tens of thousands of passengers just hours before the Paris Olympics opening ceremony.READ MORE

NTA Releases Revised Scorecard Of NEET-UG 2024 Exam; Details Here

The National Testing Agency (NTA) has released the revised scorecard of the NEET-UG 2024 examination.READ MORE

5 Sunehri Bagh: Rahul Gandhi’s New Address After 20 Years As He Dumps ‘Inauspicious’ Tughlaq Lane Bungalow

After living in 12 Tughlak Lane bungalow for the past 20 years, Congress Member of Parliament (MP) Rahul Gandhi is set to move into a new official residence – 5 Sunehri Bagh — in New Delhi. The Rae Bareli MP, who is also the leader of Opposition, has picked this home from the three-four options given to him. A letter has already been submitted to the government, giving his approval for the type eight bungalow.READ MORE

‘We Have Got Your Back’: Barack And Michelle Obama Endorse Kamala Harris For US President

Days after Joe Biden dropped out of the 2024 White House race, former US President Barack Obama endorsed Kamala Harris’ candidacy for American president on Friday.READ MORE

Kush Shah QUITS Taarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah After 16 Years, THIS Actor Is New ‘Goli’

In a surprising turn of events, Kush Shah, who essayed the character Goli in Taarak Mehta Ka Ooltah Chashmah, has quit the show. The makers have shared a video in which they introduced the new actor who will be playing his role. Kush Shah is seen saying goodbye to viewers and thanking them for showering so much love.READ MORE

Swipe Left For Next Video

SL vs PAK Women’s Asia Cup 2024 Semi Final, Live Score and Updates From Rangiri Dambulla International Stadium

Hello and welcome to the second semi-final clash of the Women’s Asia Cup 2024 where the hosts Sri Lanka take on Pakistan at the Rangiri Dambulla International Stadium, Dambulla, Sri Lanka. The fight for the second spot in the big finale is on the cards and the home team, Sri Lanka will be deemed favourites. The skipper Chamari Athapaththu will be eager to be amongst the runs and take back her spot as the leading run-scorer of the tournament.CHECK LIVE UPDATES
